issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users without replacing it, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset, which does not render a visible focus ring. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements (inputs, selects, textareas, etc.) that rely on this base style, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355-358: Replace with visible focus styles such as `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or add `box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);` alongside the existing outline-offset.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly removes the outline with outline: none and provides no visible alternative (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). This leaves buttons without any focus indicator unless they have additional overriding classes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425-428: Add a visible focus ring such as `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property (for the :active state translateY effect), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block or create a new one: `.btn { transition: none; }` or specifically `transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast);` (removing transform and box-shadow transitions).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the target difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1388-1389: Increase dimensions to meet minimum 24px: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link has a transform transition that moves the element when it appears, but there is no reduced motion media query to suppress this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add to @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): `.skip-link { transition: none; }`.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button has a min-height of 32px, which falls between 24px and 43px. While this meets the minimum size, it is below the 44px recommended target size and may be difficult for some users to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 408: Increase minimum height to 44px: `min-height: 44px;` or ensure padding provides sufficient touch area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 36px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Combined with reduced padding, this may present difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 481: Increase minimum height to 44px: `min-height: 44px;`.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size, potentially causing activation difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 608-609: Increase dimensions to 44px: `width: 44px; height: 44px;`.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended target size for comfortable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1413: Increase minimum height to 44px: `min-height: 44px;` or add sufficient padding to achieve 44px touch area.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, clip: auto, and visible overflow, which makes the content visible to sighted users and potentially hides it from screen readers depending on browser implementation. It fails to properly hide content visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378-389: Replace with correct screen-reader-only styles: `position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: This button variant includes a transform transition (translateY on hover), but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the spatial movement for users who need reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add to @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): `.hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` (removing transform).

Keyboard users navigating through the page will not see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below recommended size

issuesFound.title: Button focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for button elements. This prevents keyboard users from identifying when a button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size

issuesFound.title: Focus-ring-inset utility removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only sets outline-offset: -3px but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. No outline width or alternative visual indicator is defined, leaving no focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only selector uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which keeps the element visible in the document flow instead of visually hiding it while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This contradicts the class name and intended purpose.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring alternative. Keyboard users navigating the page cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 391: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Body text negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative word-spacing reduces the visual separation between words, making text harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 129: `word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see focus within dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class incorrectly configured
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which keeps the content visible in the document flow. This utility should hide content visually while keeping it available to assistive technologies, but instead renders it visible to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 434-445: Replace with `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on interaction. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform animation, which may cause vestibular issues for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: Remove `transform var(--transition-fast),` from the transition property, or add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has an explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of 28×28px. This is below the 44×44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 648: `width: 44px; height: 44px;` or add `padding: 8px;` to achieve minimum 44px target

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links lack non-colour underline differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector removes text-decoration and the hover state only changes color. Users who cannot perceive colour differences cannot identify these as links or distinguish them from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 671: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to the hover state

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 655: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Product card description insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline) uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white with ~10.5px font size, giving ~2.3:1 contrast, below the required 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1576: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline and provides no alternative visual indicator. Users in high-contrast mode lose focus visibility entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 406: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Search input focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with zero offset and no visible alternative. While :focus has a box-shadow, :focus-visible specifically removes all indicators, breaking focus visibility for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 643: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, lacking a visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot track focus in the account navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 673: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Small button variant touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these small buttons on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 866: `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are frequently used and must meet minimum touch target requirements.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has explicit width and height of 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Checkboxes are interactive elements that must be large enough to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav a selector sets min-height: 24px with no padding, resulting in a touch target of exactly 24px. While this meets the absolute minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transform animation lacks reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn (min‑height 32px, padding ≈7.5px)
issuesFound.explanation: .btn has a min‑height of 32 px and vertical padding of about 7.5 px, giving an effective touch area smaller than the WCAG 44 × 44 px minimum, which hampers finger navigation on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 708: min-height: 44px; /* increase to meet minimum */ /* optionally keep padding */

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min‑height 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm’s min‑height of 20 px is well below the required 44 px touch target, making small buttons difficult to activate on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 819: min-height: 44px; /* raise to meet WCAG minimum */

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle (min‑height 28px, min‑width 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle is only 28 × 28 px with insufficient padding, falling short of the 44 × 44 px target size required for reliable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 889: min-height: 44px; Line 890: min-width: 44px; /* increase to meet WCAG minimum */

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"] (width 28px, height 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is only 28 × 28 px, which is smaller than the WCAG‑recommended 44 × 44 px touch area, making it hard to tap on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: width: 44px; Line 1063: height: 44px; /* enlarge to meet minimum */

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide the element from assistive technologies; it merely makes it visible. This defeats the purpose of a screen‑reader‑only utility.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the block with a proper technique, e.g.:  
Line 470: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability (body)
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em; which compresses word gaps and reduces legibility for all users, especially those with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: word-spacing: normal; /* remove negative spacing */

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative (a)
issuesFound.explanation: All anchor elements have text-decoration: none; removing the default underline, and no other visual cue (color change, border, background) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a non‑color visual differentiator, e.g.:  
Line 253: a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; } /* underline via border */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img rule animates transform on hover, creating motion, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, so users who request reduced motion still experience the animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override:  
Line 1118: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value uses color: #ffffff (white) against a gradient background including #bb88ee (light purple). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.6:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (even at 48px/3rem, this fails the 3:1 threshold for large text).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users on all interactive elements that do not have specific overrides.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by causing words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 329: 
  word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. In forced-colors mode, box-shadow is often suppressed, making the outline essential for visibility. No alternative focus indicator is provided.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties (perspective rotation) that creates spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Icon buttons are frequently used and must meet minimum touch target sizes.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change) for many link instances in the document. While some specific link types have underline on hover (like .feature-card a and footer-bottom links), navigation links in #nav-menu, .social-links, and footer nav rely solely on colour change, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible only sets outline-offset: -3px but does not restore a visible outline (the global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none). This results in no focus indicator for elements using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator. While other inputs have box-shadow on :focus, this specific checkbox focus-visible rule overrides without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with outline-offset but provide no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes set width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Custom checkboxes must meet minimum size requirements or have sufficient spacing to adjacent targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, which falls below the 44px recommended size and has no compensating padding to increase the effective touch area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no padding specified, meeting the minimum 24px but falling below the 44px recommended target size without compensating spacing.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from screen readers (though it is intended to be hidden visually). More critically, it fails to use the standard clip/absolute positioning technique, causing the content to be visible and disrupt layout, while potentially being hidden by display: none in specific instances (like line 1089). The class fails to properly hide content visually while keeping it accessible.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) against the same light purple gradient background (#bb88ee). This results in extremely poor contrast well below the 4.5:1 minimum required for 14px text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section h2
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section heading uses color: #ffffff against a gradient background from #5588bb to #bb88ee. Against the lighter purple (#bb88ee), the contrast ratio is approximately 2.6:1, failing WCAG AA requirements for large text (3:1).

issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` and only an `outline-offset`, removing the default focus ring without providing any visible replacement (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.

Line 437: `:focus-visible {`

Line 439: `  outline-offset: 3px;`  
Line 440: `  border-radius: var(--radius-sm);`  
Line 441: `}`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state on `.hero-image img` applies a 3‑D `transform` (rotateY/rotateX) that moves the image, but there is no corresponding override inside a `@media (prefers-reduced‑motion: reduce)` block, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

Line 1415: `    transform: none;`

Line 1417: `  }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement, leaving all button elements without visible focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement, hiding focus from the billing period toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1710: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement, leaving filter buttons without visible focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1760: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, making the checkbox touch target smaller than 24px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible and removes it from the accessibility tree incorrectly. It should hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transform transition that creates spatial movement, but the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition, creating motion that may trigger vestibular disorders, but no reduced-motion override is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(-200%); } .skip-link:focus { transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on hero heading
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-content h1 selector sets letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), excessively compressing the main page heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 680: .hero-content h1 { font-size: var(--font-size-6xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-extrabold); line-height: var(--line-height-none); letter-spacing: normal; color: var(--colour-neutral-900); text-wrap: balance; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .feature-card a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum required for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Button active state transform missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) without a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on skip link

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text will be visible to all users, breaking the layout and design.

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation missing reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link underline removed with no alternative

issuesFound.title: Social link underline removed with no alternative

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing any visible focus style such as a box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline. Keyboard‑only users lose the focus indicator, making navigation impossible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 274: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their default focus ring because .btn:focus-visible sets outline: none with no visual replacement, leaving keyboard users without a focus cue.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links that expand the entire feature card have outline removed and no alternative focus style, breaking keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons hide the focus outline without a replacement, preventing users who rely on keyboard focus from seeing which question is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its focus outline and provides no alternative, making it invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons lose their focus indicator with no replacement, hindering keyboard navigation of the product filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons remove the outline without any visible focus style, making it impossible for keyboard users to know which pricing option is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links hide the focus outline and provide no alternative, breaking keyboard navigation to external profiles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the focus outline without replacement, preventing keyboard users from locating the brand link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1460: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links hide the focus outline and lack any visual cue, making them invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1475: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the focus outline without providing an alternative, so keyboard users cannot see when the table region has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20 px with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than the WCAG 44 px recommended size, which hampers finger navigation on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: .btn-sm { min-height: 20px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); /* increase vertical padding to at least 8 px */ padding-top: var(--space-2); padding-bottom: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses spacing between words and reduces readability for screen‑reader users and people with dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule “a { text-decoration: none; }” removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (border, background, or font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img defines a transition on transform (spatial movement) but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block does not disable or override this transition, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 950: .hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }
  Line 970: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-card--featured:hover applies a transform (translateY and scale) without a reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1380: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }
  Line 1390: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 670: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid() function, before the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer and emits a custom event, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a FAQ accordion item, the function animates the panel height and adds CSS classes but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the answer panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2560: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button (SELECTORS.navToggle). This leaves screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.openInstance missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu.openInstance() adds the “open” class to a dropdown panel but does not set aria‑expanded="true" on the associated trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.open = true; add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); in openInstance().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an FAQ answer panel but never updates aria‑expanded="true" on the question button, so screen readers cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Insert setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when the item is opened.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.openInstance lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu updates the UI but does not announce the change to a screen‑reader via an aria‑live region, leaving users unaware of the new menu content.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} opened`); or update a dedicated status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.closeInstance lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu updates the UI without announcing the closure to assistive technologies, causing hidden state to be missed.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} closed`); or update a live region.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation missing aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form validates the email field manually but never sets aria‑invalid="true"/"false" on the <input type="email"> when the value is invalid/valid, so screen‑reader users receive no error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _onSubmit(), after the validation check, add setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); on error and removeAttribute('aria-invalid') on success.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.openInstance missing live‑region announcement (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: The openInstance() function changes visible content (shows a submenu) but does not write to a dedicated aria‑live region, violating the requirement that each user‑initiated visible change be announced.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, update a status element with aria‑live="polite", e.g., statusEl.textContent = `${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} menu opened`;.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.closeInstance missing live‑region announcement (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a submenu changes the UI without a corresponding update to an aria‑live region, so assistive technology users are not informed of the closure.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, set statusEl.textContent = `${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} menu closed`; where statusEl has aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.openInstance missing aria‑expanded update (duplicate element for separate issue tracking)
issuesFound.explanation: The same openInstance() function also fails to set aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger, a distinct accessibility requirement from the live‑region issue.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1029: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); before addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function not updating aria-expanded (mobileNav.open)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after _open = true;

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced (mobileNav.close)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation menu but does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); after removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function not updating aria-expanded (dropdownMenu._openInstance)
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced (dropdownMenu._closeInstance)
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function collapses the dropdown panel but does not announce the action via a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); after emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function not updating aria-expanded (faqAccordion.open)
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced (faqAccordion.close)
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the FAQ answer but does not announce the collapsed state via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the answer is now hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2274: Add liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', truncate(item.question, 60))); after removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: Toggle buttons missing aria-pressed (productFilter tabs)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons in productFilter act as toggle buttons (one active at a time) but the _syncTabAriaStates function only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button and aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons, leaving screen reader users unaware of the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1894: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and announces the state, but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); at the start of mobileNav.open() (e.g., Line 1155: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not announce the closing action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes classes and hides the drawer but never announces that the navigation has been closed, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer is hidden, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosedAnnouncement || 'Navigation menu closed'); (e.g., Line 1190: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosedAnnouncement || 'Navigation menu closed');`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the open class and shows the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance() (e.g., Line 1520: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing a dropdown changes visible content but no aria‑live region is updated, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); (e.g., Line 1525 and Line 1545).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded attribute (default false)
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse() creates trigger elements but never sets aria‑expanded to false when the item is initially collapsed, violating the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After creating trigger, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); (e.g., Line 2075: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and announces the opening but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside open() (e.g., Line 2190: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid by adding error messages but never sets aria‑invalid="true", so screen readers do not convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate() after detecting an invalid result, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and on valid state remove the attribute (e.g., Line 3325 and Line 3335).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes error text but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the same validation routine, after a successful validation, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); (e.g., Line 3335).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle functions do not announce state changes via a live region (open/close)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates the UI but does not write to a live region, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} — expanded`); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} — collapsed`); (e.g., Line 2195 and Line 2205).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not announce the closing action via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown updates the UI but provides no live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); (e.g., Line 1540).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function associates an error message element with a field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the markInvalid function, after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open function reveals the mobile navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger), leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu is open. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetric state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1973: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the _open = true; assignment.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content of the page, but neither function writes to an ARIA live region to announce the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2058: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); after the emit call in _openInstance.
  Line 2078: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); after the emit call in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (inst.trigger), violating the ARIA disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2053: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ accordion item adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the answer panel, but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true", preventing assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3524: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate the active category via CSS classes, but the _syncTabAriaStates function does not expose the selected state to assistive technologies using aria-pressed (or aria-selected), making it impossible for screen reader users to know which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3124: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop, after the toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (open) – mobile navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function makes the navigation drawer visible but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (close) – mobile navigation
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() correctly sets aria‑expanded="false", but the corresponding open() function never sets it to true, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure mobileNav.open() includes setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); so the attribute is toggled both ways.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (open) – dropdown menu
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds a CSS class, but does not set aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); to reflect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (open) – dropdown menu keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: In _dropdownMenu._onTriggerKeydown() the menu is opened on Enter/Space/ArrowDown but the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to true.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance(inst) add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); so keyboard activation updates the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (open) – FAQ accordion
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the question button.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region – dropdown menu state changes
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus updates visible UI (adds/removes hidden and CSS classes) but no aria‑live region is updated to announce the change to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance() and _closeInstance() call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened') or ('Menu closed') respectively.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid – newsletter email field
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']); adds visual error styling but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the <input> when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult() add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') when result.valid is false.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not cleared from aria‑invalid – newsletter email field
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter email field becomes valid, inlineValidator._renderResult() removes visual error classes but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the attribute stale.
issuesFound.fix: In the valid branch of _renderResult() call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); to clear the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Hidden drawer not marked with aria‑hidden when closed – mobile navigation
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the drawer by removing CSS classes but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, so the off‑screen menu remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); to hide it from assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑controls reference – mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.init() sets aria‑controls to _drawer.id even though the drawer element has no id attribute, resulting in a reference to a non‑existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer';) or generate one before assigning aria‑controls.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking form fields invalid, the function updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers do not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2400 (in markInvalid function): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers are unaware of the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3655 (in init function, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3750: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line 3765: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons, preventing screen readers from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3750 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3765 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Filtering products updates the visible grid and count badge, but does not announce the results to screen readers, leaving users unaware that filtering occurred or how many results exist.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4300 (in applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons do not indicate their selected state via aria-pressed (or aria-checked), making it impossible for screen reader users to determine which theme is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7200 (in set function, inside qsa callback): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === pref));` for each button

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons do not reference the product grid panel they control via aria-controls, reducing context for screen reader users about what content will be filtered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4280 (in init, after _gridEl is assigned): Add `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'product-grid')));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never assigned a createFocusTrap() instance. Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the open mobile menu, allowing users to tab to background elements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3580 (declaration): Initialize after elements found: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line 3600 (in open function): Add `_trap.activate();`
  Line 3620 (in close function): Add `_trap.deactivate();`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes aria-describedby references but fails to clear aria-invalid to "false" or remove it, leaving stale invalid states on fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410 (in markValid function): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change or new prices to screen readers, hiding important cost information from non-visual users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4850 (in setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch is assigned role="switch" but the JavaScript does not ensure it has an accessible name via aria-label, leaving screen reader users without context if the HTML lacks text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4820 (in init, after setting role): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.switchToAnnual || 'Switch billing period');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria-controls to programmatically associate it with the menu drawer it expands, reducing context for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3655 (in init): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: navKeyboardShortcuts Alt+key conflicts
issuesFound.explanation: Keyboard shortcuts using Alt+key combinations (e.g., Alt+S, Alt+N) may conflict with screen reader shortcuts, preventing users from accessing browser or assistive technology functions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: Document shortcuts visibly on the page or provide a help dialog, or change modifiers to avoid conflicts (e.g., use Ctrl+Alt).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button used to open and close the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` respectively.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The same `.nav-toggle` button toggles between open and closed states but never sets `aria‑pressed`, violating the ARIA button pattern for two‑state controls.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.toggle()` (or in `open`/`close`) call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` when opened and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')` when closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger does not reference the drawer it controls via `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer element is created, assign it an id (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), then add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden only with CSS, no aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed it is hidden by CSS (`is‑open` class) but remains in the accessibility tree because no `aria-hidden="true"` is applied.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible content but no live‑region (`role="status"`/`aria-live`) is updated, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; after closing, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `true`, breaking the accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria‑expanded false
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items that start closed have no `aria-expanded="false"` on their trigger buttons, so the initial state is ambiguous to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()` after creating `trigger`, if the item is not initially open, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑controls (broken reference)
issuesFound.explanation: Although `aria-controls` is set to the panel’s id, the panel element may not have an `id` when the FAQ is generated dynamically, leading to a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each panel receives a unique id via `srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'faq-panel')` before assigning `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription email input validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is validated manually; when the value is invalid the code shows a status message but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so screen‑reader users cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: After `if (!result.valid) { inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl); }` add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing‑period toggle (`data-billing-toggle`) changes the UI but never updates `aria-expanded`, leaving the state unknown to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick()` after toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', String(_pricingState.period !== 'month'))`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new price to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user selects a different billing period, the displayed prices change but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users may miss the update.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of `setPeriod()` add `liveRegion.announce('Pricing updated to ' + periodLabel(period))`.
