issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When rendering validation results, the function updates CSS classes and aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields, preventing screen readers from announcing field validity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250: In _renderResult, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);` before the if/else block.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles the mobile navigation drawer open but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (inst.trigger), so screen readers do not announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1870: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_open = false;` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible result count updates but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users how many products are now showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState invalid aria-selected on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons use aria-selected to indicate state, but aria-selected is not valid on button elements (only on gridcell, option, row, tab, or columnheader). The buttons should use aria-pressed or have role="tab".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3180: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` and ensure buttons have type="button".

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: After updating prices, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to ${period} billing\`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization code discovers the trigger and drawer elements but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, violating WCAG 4.1.2 requirement that the initial state be programmatically determined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but fails to announce the action completion to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` at the end of _onClick.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's id, preventing screen reader users from understanding the relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: After ensuring _drawer has an id (using srUtils.ensureId), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown panel, the function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with incorrect state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ item sets the panel to visible but fails to update aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger), violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in the open function after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing a FAQ item does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, causing screen readers to report the item as still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3570: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the close function after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function declares a _trap variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap when opening the drawer, allowing focus to escape to background elements while the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: After opening the drawer, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing `aria‑controls` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer element (`SELECTORS.navDrawer`). Without this relationship AT cannot associate the control with the region it shows/hides.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger` and `_drawer`, add:  

```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));
```

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the code toggles CSS classes and visibility of the panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state. AT therefore cannot know whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Within `_openInstance()` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; within `_closeInstance()` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `faqAccordion.close(item)` functions animate the panel but never set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger element (`item.trigger`). Users of assistive technology cannot perceive the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds a descriptive error element but never sets the native `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the field. Screen readers therefore do not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `inlineValidator.validate()` after a failed validation, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form email field validation not reflected in `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<input>` in the newsletter form is bound with `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required','email'])`, but because the inline validator does not set `aria-invalid`, the field never receives the required ARIA state when the user enters an invalid address.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields validation not reflected in `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs are bound with `inlineValidator.bind()` but, as with the newsletter form, the validator never sets `aria-invalid`. Errors are only shown visually.
issuesFound.fix:
Apply the same fix as Issue 5 so each bound field receives `aria-invalid="true"` on error and the attribute is removed on success.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not announce state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed, the code emits custom events (`dropdown:open`, `dropdown:close`) but does not write a message to any element with `role="status"` or `aria-live`. Users of AT are not informed that a submenu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening or closing, update a hidden live‑region element, e.g.:  

```js
liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} menu ${inst.open ? 'opened' : 'closed'}`);
```

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not announce state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` and `close()` emit custom events but never write to a live‑region. AT users are not notified when an answer panel expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live‑region announcement similar to:  

```js
liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} ${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}`);
```

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce period changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates the displayed prices but does not provide an audible cue for screen‑reader users that the billing period has changed (e.g., from monthly to annual).
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, call:  

```js
liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);
```

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing `aria‑pressed` (should use `aria‑checked` for a switch) – currently only `aria‑checked` is set, but the visual toggle is a button that behaves like a switch and should also have `role="switch"` and `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data-billing-toggle` is given `role="switch"` and `aria‑checked`, but the inner button that users click does not have `aria‑pressed`, causing inconsistent state exposure.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear))` alongside `aria‑checked`, or change the inner button to use `aria‑pressed`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class only, not via `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`SELECTORS.navDrawer`) is hidden by removing the `is-open` class, but no `aria‑hidden` attribute is toggled. AT may still expose the drawer content when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with `hidden` attribute but not with `aria‑hidden`; while `hidden` is sufficient, the live‑region announcements (Issue 8) are missing, so the lack of ARIA state is compounded.
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via `panel.setAttribute('hidden','')` but AT may not announce the change without `aria‑hidden`.
issuesFound.fix:
When hiding, also set `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden','true')`; when showing, remove it.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels are hidden with `hidden` attribute but not with `aria‑hidden`, and no live‑region announcement (Issue 9) – same as above.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` on close and remove on open.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks a visible focus indicator when focus is moved via keyboard (focus‑visible is provided but the button also has `outline: none` in its CSS without an alternative).
issuesFound.explanation: The button may have `outline: none` without a visible alternative, violating focus‑indicator guidelines.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the button’s focus style includes a visible `box-shadow` or `border` in the same rule block, e.g.:  

```css
.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--focus-color); }
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid, after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded update in open function
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and emits an event, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open, after the line `_open = true;`, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via the liveRegion announcer, leaving users unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of pricing.setPeriod, add: `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed to {period}').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded update in close function
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the mobile navigation drawer, but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers will continue to report the menu as expanded after it closes.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, before calling `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close'...`, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the visual state changes and aria-expanded is updated, but no confirmation is announced to screen readers via the liveRegion, so users may not realize the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open, after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add: `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqOpened || '{title} answer expanded').replace('{title}', item.title));`. In faqAccordion.close, after `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add: `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} answer collapsed').replace('{title}', item.title));`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded in _openInstance
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu, the function updates the panel visibility and CSS classes but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true".
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._openInstance, after `inst.open = true;`, add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Toast dismiss animation missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When dismissing a toast notification, the element remains in the DOM during the CSS hide animation. If CSS uses opacity or transform rather than display:none, the toast remains accessible to screen readers during the animation. The function should immediately hide the element from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In toastManager.dismiss, at the beginning of the function, add: `setAttr(toastEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded in _closeInstance
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown menu, the function updates the panel state but does not set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false".
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;`, add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (should be set to "false" or removed), leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markValid, at the end of the function, add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Search input combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the listbox is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In suggestionListRenderer.mount, after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add: `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons showing active/inactive states, but the code only updates CSS classes and does not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter._syncTabAriaStates, inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Product filter result count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count badge is updated, the change is not announced to screen readers, so users do not know how many products match the current filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter._updateCountBadge, at the end of the function, add: `liveRegion.announce(String(count) + ' product' + (count !== 1 ? 's' : '') + ' found');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions _openInstance and _closeInstance in the dropdownMenu controller change the visibility of a dropdown panel but never set or update the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions in the faqAccordion module set aria‑hidden on the panel but never update aria‑expanded on the question button. Assistive technologies therefore lack the state of the accordion item.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in close(item) call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu dynamic content not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened or closed, no text is written to a live‑region element. Users of screen readers receive no audible cue that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: After opening or closing, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened') or liveRegion.announce('Menu closed') or update a dedicated role="status" element.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate function marks fields as invalid by adding error messages and CSS classes, but it never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the offending input (and never removes it on success). Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In validate when result.valid is false, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and when result.valid is true, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid').

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger button missing aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button used to open the mobile navigation drawer is never given an aria‑expanded attribute when the page loads (its default state is closed). Screen‑reader users cannot know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init or immediately after selecting _trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer hidden via CSS without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden only by removing a CSS class (and by CSS transforms). No aria‑hidden attribute is applied, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and can be read by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when hiding and remove the attribute when showing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu user action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a dropdown trigger changes visible content (opens a menu) but no live‑region announcement is made, violating the requirement that user‑initiated UI changes be announced.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance or _closeInstance, write a brief status message to an element with role="status" or call liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened') / 'Dropdown closed'.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid on invalid form fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid(): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions omit aria-expanded update on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, leaving users unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`
    In mobileNav.close(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period toggle buttons omit aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (_periodBtns) function as a toggle button group where one button is active at a time. The function updates the CSS active class but fails to set aria-pressed to reflect the pressed state. Screen readers announce the buttons as plain buttons rather than toggle buttons with state.
issuesFound.fix:
In _syncToggleState(): Replace the forEach loop body with:
    `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions omit aria-expanded update on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened or closed, the functions manipulate the panel's hidden attribute and CSS classes but fail to toggle aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
    In _closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid on valid form fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" (or does not explicitly set it to "false"). Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markValid(): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function (after the if/else block that updates aria-describedby).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu omits aria-expanded initialisation on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state. Screen readers cannot convey the initial collapsed state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register(): After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks aria-hidden when positioned off-screen
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, it is typically positioned off-screen via CSS (e.g., transform: translateX) rather than using display:none. The close() function does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer (_drawer), leaving it exposed to screen readers while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close(): After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
    In mobileNav.open(): After `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch omits aria-expanded initialisation on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input functions as a combobox with a collapsed suggestion list by default, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load. Screen readers cannot inform users that the field has a collapsed popup.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch.init(): After `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl));`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function omits aria-expanded update on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the open() function sets item.open = true and animates the panel height but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). Although the close() function sets it to false, the missing update in open() leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox omits aria-expanded updates when suggestion list opens or closes
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion listbox. The _open() and _close() functions manage the internal _searchState.open flag and listbox visibility but never toggle aria-expanded on the input element (_inputEl). Screen readers cannot inform users when the suggestion list is displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
In siteSearch._open(): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`
    In siteSearch._close(): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav omits aria-expanded initialisation on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed by default when the page loads, but the trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Screen readers announce the button without indicating that it controls a collapsed menu.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(): After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open fails to move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer reveals new interactive content, but focus remains on the trigger button. Keyboard users must manually navigate into the drawer. Focus should move to the first focusable element in the drawer or to the drawer itself.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open(): After `_lockScroll();`, add:
    `requestAnimationFrame(() => { const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true }); });`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs lack role and keyboard activation handling
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab bar uses roving tabindex for arrow key navigation, but the tab elements ([data-filter-value]) may not be native buttons. The code does not ensure they have role="button" (or role="tab") and does not handle Enter/Space key activation for non-button elements. Keyboard users cannot activate filters with Enter or Space if the elements are divs or spans.
issuesFound.fix:
In productFilter.init(): After creating the roving tabindex, add:
    `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { if (btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(btn, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(btn, 'tabindex', '-1'); } btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation drawer opens or closes, no announcement is made via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu state has changed. Users may not realize the menu is now open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open(): After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    In mobileNav.close(): After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes the visible UI but provides no live region announcement. Screen reader users may not be aware that a submenu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance(): After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');`
    In _closeInstance(): After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the page content height but does not announce the action via a live region. Screen reader users may not know that new content is visible or that content was hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqAccordion.open(): After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded');`
    In faqAccordion.close(): After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period switch not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the visible prices but does not announce the change via the live region. Screen reader users may not realize the pricing display has updated.
issuesFound.fix:
In pricing.setPeriod(): After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add:
    `const periodLabel = BILLING_PERIODS[period].label; liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) controls the drawer (`SELECTORS.navDrawer`), but no `aria-controls` attribute is set linking the button to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer')` during `mobileNav.init()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden only by CSS classes, not aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS (`removeClass(_drawer, …)`) but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`. The element remains in the accessibility tree, causing screen‑reader confusion.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.open()`, remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile menu triggers visual changes and emits custom events, but no ARIA live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After `open()` call, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.openMenu || 'Navigation menu opened')`; after `close()`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.closeMenu || 'Navigation menu closed')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick()` the trigger button’s visibility state is toggled, but the code never updates `aria-expanded` to reflect the open/closed state of the associated panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_openInstance(inst)` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; inside `_closeInstance(inst)` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email'])` validates the field but only adds an error message element; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input>` when the value is invalid. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Extend `inlineValidator` to call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on validation failure and remove it on success, or add a custom rule that does this.

---

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields lack aria‑invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs (`full_name`, `email`, `subject`, `message`, `consent`) are bound with `inlineValidator`, which does not set `aria-invalid`. Consequently, screen‑reader users are not informed of which fields are invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Modify `inlineValidator` (or each field’s validation) to set `aria-invalid="true"` when the field fails validation and remove it when the field becomes valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions do not clear aria‑invalid on valid input (medium severity)
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, `inlineValidator` removes the error message but does not clear `aria-invalid`. The attribute remains, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
In the success branch of `inlineValidator._renderResult`, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` and `close()` functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change to a live region. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback when an answer panel expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded')`; after closing, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria-controls on trigger buttons (when not auto‑generated)
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse()`, the code ensures `aria-controls` is set on the trigger, but if a trigger already has an `id` but no `aria-controls`, the attribute may be missing. This breaks the relationship between the button and its panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure every trigger receives `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)` regardless of existing attributes.

---

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion list items lack role="option" and aria‑selected updates (medium severity)
issuesFound.explanation: While the suggestion list is given `role="listbox"` and items have `role="option"`, the code does not update `aria-selected` on the active item (only toggles a CSS class). Assistive technologies rely on `aria-selected` to convey the current selection.
issuesFound.fix:
In `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex()`, add `setAttr(item, 'aria-selected', String(isActive))` for each option.

---

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() helper links error messages via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Conversely, markValid() does not clear aria-invalid. Assistive technologies cannot identify invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1524: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in markInvalid before or after addDescribedBy.
  Line 1535: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` in markValid, or use `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions open() and close() do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the visibility state of the drawer. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1818: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the start of the open function after the guard clause.
  Line 1834: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the close function after the guard clause.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function sets it to false, the mismatch means the expanded state is never announced as true when opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2226: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after setting item.open = true.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button (_trigger) to indicate the closed state of the drawer on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1872: After the line `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function changes billing period prices visibly but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the price values have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2072: At the end of setPeriod(), insert `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);` (or use STRINGS.pricingMonthly/Annual).

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function in the pricing module updates CSS active classes for billing period buttons but does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or "false" on inactive buttons. Screen readers cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2092: Inside the forEach loop over _periodBtns, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: During dropdown registration in init(), the trigger button receives aria-haspopup but not an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute to reflect that the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1984: In the register() function (called from init), after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch controller functions _open() and _close() toggle the suggestion listbox visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the combobox input (_inputEl) to reflect whether the popup is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1300: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` in _open after setting _searchState.open = true.
  Line 1309: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` in _close after setting _searchState.open = false.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" but does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute to indicate the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1348: After the line `_inputEl = qs(cfg.inputSelector);` add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or the first focusable element inside it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must manually navigate into the newly opened menu.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the _openInstance function does not move focus into the panel (either to the panel itself or the first menu item). Keyboard users remain on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` in the dropdown menu controller adds the “open” class to the panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation button missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle (`_trigger`) receives `aria-controls` when opened/closed via `setExpanded`, but its initial state is never set to `aria-expanded="false"`, causing ambiguity for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init`, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions emit custom events but do not provide an audible announcement of the navigation drawer’s state, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.openMenu || 'Navigation menu opened');` on open and a corresponding “closed” message on close.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) changes the pricing period but never updates `aria-pressed` to reflect its on/off state, violating ARIA button patterns.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick`, after changing the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds visual error classes but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the invalid field, so screen readers do not learn that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: When `!result.valid`, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not cleared from aria-invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes visual classes but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the valid branch of `_renderResult`, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Product count badge updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_updateCountBadge` updates the numeric badge but does not provide an audible announcement, so users of assistive technology cannot know how many products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown fires custom events but does not write to an `aria-live` region, leaving users unaware of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix: After `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', ...)` also call `liveRegion.announce(trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true' ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens/closes without any live‑region announcement, so users cannot perceive the change.
issuesFound.fix: After `setExpanded(_trigger, true/false)`, call `liveRegion.announce(…);` with appropriate “Navigation menu opened/closed” messages.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change, so screen‑reader users cannot know when an answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`);` and in `close(item)` announce “… answer collapsed”.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2780: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2800: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu is not announced via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2550: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
  Line 2570: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but the _open() and _close() functions do not toggle aria-expanded to reflect the visibility state of the suggestions listbox.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3150: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 3170: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu open and close actions are not announced via the live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2780: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` immediately before the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`
  Line 2800: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` immediately before the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: pricing switch toggle missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element (_toggleEl) has role="switch" but the code never updates aria-checked to reflect the selected state (monthly vs annual).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3870: Inside the _syncToggleState function, after `const isYear = period === 'year';` and before the closing brace of the if-block, insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));`
  Line 3930: Inside the init function, after `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);`, insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(initPeriod === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is not announced, so screen reader users do not know that the results have changed or how many items are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: Inside the applyFilters function, within the filterAnimation.run callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, leaving fields incorrectly marked as invalid after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1860: At the end of the markValid function (before the final closing brace), insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, nor does it set aria-controls to link the trigger to the drawer panel.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing dialog semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is treated as a modal dialog but lacks role="dialog", aria-modal="true", and an accessible name (aria-label).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is initialised as a combobox but aria-expanded is not set to "false" on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3220: After the line `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates the visual prices but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: Inside the setPeriod function, after the line `requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pricingState.animating = false; });`, insert `liveRegion.announce(`Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, _openInstance adds the “open” class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, breaking accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true') when opening and setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons toggle open/closed states without updating aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance/_closeInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true') or 'false'.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons toggle answer panels but never set aria‑pressed, making the toggle state ambiguous for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In open() and close() add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true') or 'false'.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters updates product count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count badge is updated, but no aria‑live region is used, so users of assistive technology are not informed of the new count.
issuesFound.fix: Create a hidden live region (e.g., <div role="status" aria-live="polite">) and set its textContent to “X products shown” after each filter run.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not announce panel expansion via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening an FAQ item updates the UI but does not provide an audible cue; no aria‑live announcement is made.
issuesFound.fix: After expanding, call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`) or update a dedicated status region.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are created with aria-haspopup but no initial aria‑expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false').

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons have aria-controls but no initial aria‑expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse set setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false') when the item is not initially open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close hides drawer via CSS class without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the element is hidden by removing the “is‑open” class, but no aria‑hidden is applied, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true') and remove it on open().

issuesFound.title: product filter count change not announced via live region (duplicate of Issue 7 but for user‑action reporting)
issuesFound.explanation: Users changing filter tabs, search query, or sort order see the count badge update, but there is no dedicated live‑region announcement for each action.
issuesFound.fix: After each filter change, update a live region with a message like “3 products shown after filtering”.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing‑period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly, quarterly, or annual billing updates UI prices but does not inform screen‑reader users of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period set to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling error states, but it does not map this to aria-invalid on the underlying input element. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify when the field has failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 427: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls linking it to the answer panel, but it does not have aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state. Assistive technology users cannot tell if the answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1268: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing errorId and errorMessage props
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup is passed the error prop (which it does not accept) instead of errorMessage, and errorId is omitted. Consequently, the ErrorMessage component is never rendered, and the aria-describedby on the email input references a non-existent ID, breaking the association between the field and its error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1372: Change error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} and add errorId="newsletter-email-error".

issuesFound.explanation: The reusable TextInput component never forwards the “invalid” prop to the native input as aria‑invalid, so error states are not announced to AT.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing contextual accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The plan CTA link uses generic text like "Get started" or "Talk to sales" without an aria-label. When multiple plan cards are present, screen reader users cannot distinguish which plan each button corresponds to.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second list of integrations is a visual clone for seamless looping animation, but it is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1054: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true". Screen readers may not treat the dialog as modal, allowing users to navigate outside the panel while it is open.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal misplaced
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". The dialog itself must have aria-modal to correctly signal modality to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element containing skip links, but the nav lacks an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, users cannot distinguish the purpose of this navigation block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 329: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the navigation dropdown does not have an aria-expanded attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open. This affects users of assistive technology who rely on ARIA state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes the mobile navigation panel lacks aria-expanded, so AT cannot convey its open/closed state. Affects keyboard and screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile submenu trigger buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that expand sub‑menus in the mobile navigation have aria-controls but no aria-expanded, leaving AT users unaware of their state. Affects all AT users.

issuesFound.title: Hero section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> that contains the hero content has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, so screen‑reader users cannot identify the region. Affects all AT users.

Line 543: >

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons used to filter products do not convey their selected state via aria-pressed, leaving AT users unaware of the active filter. Affects screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button (Scanning) missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 8 for the “Scanning” tab button.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element (see Issue 8 fix).

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button (Design) missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 8 for the “Design” tab button.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element (see Issue 8 fix).

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button (Testing) missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 8 for the “Testing” tab button.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element (see Issue 8 fix).

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button (Reporting) missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 8 for the “Reporting” tab button.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element (see Issue 8 fix).

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle “Monthly” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button does not indicate its pressed state via aria-pressed, so AT cannot tell which option is active. Affects screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle “Annual” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button lacks aria-pressed, so AT cannot know which period is selected. Affects screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Hero video play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles background video playback does not use aria-pressed to convey its state, leaving AT users unaware of whether the video is playing. Affects screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation link “Products” missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link that represents the current page does not have aria-current="page", so AT cannot identify the active page. Affects screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the newsletter email sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists, breaking the reference. Affects screen‑reader users who cannot hear the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Add an element with id="newsletter-email-error" (e.g., render <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={...} />) or remove the aria-describedby attribute.

issuesFound.title: Contact form message textarea aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea’s aria-describedby includes "contact-message-error", which is not rendered, breaking the reference. Affects AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Render an error element with id="contact-message-error" when needed, or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: TextInput not propagating aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an invalid prop to indicate error state but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute on the underlying input element. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls the visibility of a submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The second (cloned) list of integration logos used for visual looping is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as toggle buttons to select a category but do not communicate their selected state via aria-pressed (or aria-selected). Screen readers cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial slides that are not currently visible remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to access content that is not visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle button controls the visibility of the navigation panel but lacks aria-expanded to indicate the panel's state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an element ID 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when an error occurs, but the ErrorMessage component is rendered with id 'newsletter-status'. This mismatch means screen readers cannot locate and announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1618: `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-status' : undefined}`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing programmatic association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component accepts an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between the label text and the input field, preventing screen readers from announcing the label when the field is focused.

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk (*) that marks required fields is rendered as visible text without aria‑hidden, causing screen readers to announce a stray asterisk.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1589: * (Repeat the same fix for every required‑field marker in the NewsletterForm, ContactForm, and any other FormGroup with required={true}.)

issuesFound.title: Text inputs do not expose invalid state via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component forwards an “invalid” prop but never maps it to aria‑invalid, so when a field is in error AT is not notified.

issuesFound.title: Form fields missing aria‑describedby linking to error messages
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput includes an aria‑describedby that points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id is ever rendered, so the relationship is broken and error text is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an id to the ErrorMessage rendered for the email field, e.g.:
  In NewsletterForm, replace the ErrorMessage usage with:
  <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} />

  Similarly, in ContactForm each TextInput should reference the corresponding error message id rendered by FormGroup. Adjust FormGroup usage to pass errorId prop matching the input’s id, e.g.:
  <FormGroup
    label="Full name"
    htmlFor="contact-name"
    required
    errorId="contact-name-error"
    errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  >
    <TextInput
      id="contact-name"
      …
      aria-describedby="contact-name-error"
    />
  </FormGroup>

(Apply analogous fixes for email, subject, and message fields.)

issuesFound.title: Buttons with loading state do not expose aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button receives a “loading” prop (which the Button component does not recognize) and therefore never receives aria‑busy, leaving AT unaware that the button is busy.

issuesFound.title: Spinner component not hidden from AT when used as decorative loading indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner rendered inside a loading Button is a plain <span> without aria‑hidden, so AT may announce it as content.
issuesFound.fix:
Update the Button component’s loading markup to use the Spinner component (which already has aria‑hidden) or add aria‑hidden:
  Replace:
  <span className="btn-spinner" />
  With:
  <Spinner size="sm" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation link for current page missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: The link with `className="nav-link--current"` does not have `aria-current="page"`, so AT cannot know which page is active.
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-current="page"` to the `<a>` element when `item.current` is true.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation link for current page missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 39 for the mobile navigation link.
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-current="page"` to the `<a>` element when `item.current` is true.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria‑labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: Each pricing plan’s `<article>` does not reference its heading via `aria-labelledby`, so AT cannot identify the plan name when navigating the list.
issuesFound.fix: Inside PlanCard, add `aria-labelledby={headingId}` to the `<article>` element.

issuesFound.title: Hero section <section> missing aria‑labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero `<section>` does not reference its heading (`id="hero-heading"`), leaving the region unnamed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: `<section` `className="hero-section"` `aria-labelledby="hero-heading">`

issuesFound.title: Stats bar <section> missing aria‑labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics `<section>` lacks an accessible name; it should reference the hidden `<h2 id="stats-heading">`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: `<section` `className="stats-bar"` `aria-labelledby="stats-heading">`
